title: A733 SDMMC0 GCTRL bit 0x00000800 source audit

vendor_bsp_v5p3x.finding: sunxi_mmc_set_rdtmout_reg_v5p3x() sets SDXC_DTIME_UNIT only when the computed read-data timeout exceeds SDXC_MAX_RDTO, scales the timeout by 256, writes REG_TMOUT, and clears SDXC_DTIME_UNIT when recovering REG_TMOUT.

vendor_tmout_sample.summary: During vendor working reads, GCTRL=0x20000830 appears with TMOUT=0x01312dff. Idle/recovered samples show GCTRL=0x20000010 with TMOUT=0xffffffff. This matches the BSP timeout-unit path.

mainline_h013.summary: H013 fails with GCTRL=0x20000030, TMOUT=0xffffffff, RINTR=0x24, IDST=0x4000, CHDA=DLBA, CBDA=0, CBCR=0x400, and BBCR=0. The failure is descriptor-read progress, not a data-timeout expiration.

conclusion: Do not build a magic GCTRL bit-11 diagnostic from H014. The source-backed meaning of 0x00000800 is SDXC_DTIME_UNIT, a read-data-timeout scaling bit. It explains the vendor GCTRL/TMOUT pairing but does not plausibly control IDMAC descriptor fetch. Move the next work item away from visible GCTRL/DMAC bits and toward hidden wrapper, DMA coherency, or storage-fabric evidence.
